title: Reducing errors in memory — The role of sleep
content_html:
Reducing errors in memory — The role of sleep

Source: Michigan State University News / journal Learning & Memory.

Sleep may reduce mistakes in memory, according to a first-of-its-kind study led by a scientist at Michigan State University. The findings have practical implications for many people, from students doing multiple-choice tests to elderly people confusing their medicine, says Kimberly Fenn, principal investigator and assistant professor of psychology.

'It's easy to muddle things in your mind,' Fenn says. 'This research suggests that after sleep, you're better able to pick out the incorrect parts of that memory.'

Study participants were 'trained' by being shown or listening to lists of words. Then, twelve hours later, they were shown individual words and asked to identify which words they had seen or heard in the earlier session. One group of students was trained at 10 a.m. and tested at 10 p.m. after a normal sleepless day. Another group was trained at night and tested twelve hours later in the morning, after about six hours of sleep. Three experiments were conducted. In each experiment, the results showed that students who had slept did not have as many problems with false memory and chose fewer incorrect words.

Further research is needed, she said, adding that she plans to study different population groups, particularly the elderly. 'We know older individuals generally have worse memory performance than younger individuals. We also know from other research that elderly individuals tend to be more prone to false memories,' Fenn said.

questions.question: The groups in the study saw or heard lists of words at 1 ________ times of the day.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–5

Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–J, below.
questions.explanation: EN: One group was trained at 10 a.m. (morning) and another at night (different times of the day, Option G). VI: Một nhóm được đào tạo lúc 10 giờ sáng và nhóm khác vào ban đêm (các thời điểm khác nhau trong ngày, Đáp án G).

_blockConfig.noteContent:
The groups in the study saw or heard lists of words at [1] times of the day. After [2] hours, the groups tried to identify these words correctly in a test. Before the test, one group had [3] sleep and chose the words in the evening. The other group had their test in the morning.
In three experiments, the results were [4] the groups that had slept during the experiment remembered [5] words correctly than the other groups.

questions.questionHtml:
Fenn’s Memory Experiments

The groups in the study saw or heard lists of words at 1_______ times of the day. After 2_______ hours, the groups tried to identify these words correctly in a test. Before the test, one group had 3_______ sleep and chose the words in the evening. The other group had their test in the morning.

In three experiments, the results were 4_______ the groups that had slept during the experiment remembered 5_______ words correctly than the other groups.

questions.question: After 2 ________ hours, the groups tried to identify these words correctly in a test.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states: 'Then, twelve hours later, they were shown individual words...' (Option C, 12). VI: Văn bản cho biết: 'Sau đó, 12 giờ sau, họ được xem các từ riêng lẻ...' (Đáp án C, 12).

questions.question: Before the test, one group had 3 ________ sleep and chose words in the evening.
questions.explanation: EN: One group was tested 'after a normal sleepless day' (no sleep, Option H). VI: Một nhóm đã được kiểm tra 'sau một ngày không ngủ bình thường' (không ngủ, Đáp án H).

questions.question: In three experiments, the results were 4 ________:
questions.explanation: EN: 'Three experiments were conducted. In each experiment, the results showed...' (results were the same, Option J). VI: 'Ba thí nghiệm được tiến hành. Trong mỗi thí nghiệm, kết quả đều chỉ ra...' (kết quả là như nhau, Đáp án J).

questions.question: the groups that had slept during the experiment remembered 5 ________ words correctly than the other groups.
questions.explanation: EN: Students who slept 'chose fewer incorrect words', meaning they remembered more words correctly (Option A). VI: Học sinh đi ngủ 'chọn ít từ sai hơn', nghĩa là họ nhớ nhiều từ đúng hơn (Đáp án A).
